title: cicercula sativa var. amphicarpos
intro: Cicercula sativa var. amphicarpos, commonly known as the "sensitive plant," is a perennial legume native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its flavonoid and alkaloid compounds. The health benefits of Cicercula sativa var. amphicarpos include reducing inflammation and pain, improving digestion, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat fever and skin conditions.
